tasks.name: Request the earliest morning diabetes appointment at the Main Clinic within the next 14 days
tasks.description: Schedule a new patient visit for a child with Type 1 diabetes at the Main Clinic – Downtown location, choosing the earliest available morning time slot (between 8:00 AM and 11:59 AM) within the next 14 calendar days, and submit the request form.
steps: Navigate to the clinic website homepage.
Click on the “Appointments” link in the main navigation menu at the top of the page.
On the appointments page, click the button labeled “Schedule a New Patient Visit.”
In the “Reason for Visit” dropdown field, select the option labeled “Type 1 Diabetes.”
In the “Location” selection area, choose “Main Clinic – Downtown” from the available clinic locations.
Open the date picker, navigate through the calendar to view the next 14 days, and click on the earliest date that shows any available morning appointment slots.
From the list of time options shown for that date, choose the earliest available time that falls between 8:00 AM and 11:59 AM.

Fill in the patient information fields with the following details: type “Alex Rivera” in the child’s name field, enter “03/15/2014” as the date of birth, type “Maria Rivera” in the parent/guardian name field, enter “555-123-4567” in the phone number field, and type “parent@example.com” in the email field.
Click the “Request Appointment” or similarly labeled submit button at the bottom of the form to send the appointment request.

tasks.name: Select a thyroid specialist who speaks Spanish and has 10+ years of experience
tasks.description: Find a pediatric endocrinologist who treats thyroid disorders, speaks Spanish, and has at least 10 years of experience, then open their profile and locate their main office phone number.
steps: From any page, click on the “Our Doctors” or “Providers” link in the main navigation menu.
On the doctors listing page, locate and click the filter or dropdown labeled “Conditions Treated” or “Specialty,” and select the option that includes “Thyroid Disorders.”
Find the language filter (for example, a dropdown or checkbox list labeled “Languages Spoken”) and select “Spanish.”
If available, use the “Years of Experience” or “Experience” filter to choose “10+ years” or the closest option indicating at least 10 years.
If sorting options are available, open the sort dropdown and select an option that does not conflict with the criteria, such as “A–Z by Last Name” or leave the default sorting as is.
From the filtered list of doctors, click on the first doctor whose card or listing explicitly shows “10+ years experience” or an equivalent label.
On that doctor’s profile page, scroll down until you reach the section labeled “Contact,” “Office Information,” or similar.
In that section, locate the main office phone number and highlight it with your cursor or tap it once (for example, to prepare to dial or copy it).

tasks.name: Register for the next evening insulin pump education class after 5:00 PM within 60 days
tasks.description: Find the insulin pump education class schedule, select the earliest class that starts at or after 5:00 PM within the next 60 days, and complete the online registration form for one child and one parent.
steps: From the homepage, click on the “Classes & Events,” “Education,” or similar link in the main navigation or footer.
On the classes/events page, use the search bar or filters to select or type “Insulin Pump Education Class” and apply the filter to show only that class type.
If there is a date range filter, set the start date to today and the end date to 60 days from today, then apply the date filter.
Review the resulting list of insulin pump classes and identify the earliest date where a class start time is listed as 5:00 PM or later.
Click on the title or “Register” button for that earliest qualifying class (starting at or after 5:00 PM) to open the registration page.
On the registration form, enter “Liam Carter” as the child’s name and “09/10/2015” as the child’s date of birth, and select “Type 1 Diabetes” if a condition field is provided.
In the parent/guardian section, type “Emma Carter” as the parent/guardian name, enter “555-987-6543” as the phone number, and “ecarter@example.com” as the email address.

If an attendance count field is present, set the number of attendees to “2” (one child and one parent), agree to any required consent checkbox, and click the “Submit Registration” or similarly labeled button to complete the registration.

tasks.name: Save two recent, easy-reading articles about growth hormone deficiency
tasks.description: Search the patient education library for articles about growth hormone deficiency, filter to articles published within the last 2 years and labeled as easy reading level, and add the first two matching articles to your saved resources list.
steps: Navigate to the “Patient Education,” “Resources,” or “Education Library” section from the main navigation menu.
In the library search bar, type “growth hormone deficiency” and press Enter or click the search icon to view results.
Locate and use the publication date filter to restrict results to articles from the last 2 years (for example, choose “Past 2 years” or set a start date exactly 2 years prior to today).
Find the reading level or difficulty filter (e.g., “Reading Level” or “Audience”) and select the option labeled “Easy,” “Basic,” or equivalent indicating easier reading level.
Apply the filters and scroll to the top of the filtered results list for “growth hormone deficiency.”
Click on the title of the first article in the filtered results list to open its detail page.
On the article detail page, click the “Save,” “Bookmark,” or “Add to My Resources” button/icon to add this article to your saved resources list.

Use the browser’s back button or the site’s “Back to results” link to return to the filtered results, then click on the title of the second article in the list and again click the “Save” or “Bookmark” control to save the second article.

tasks.name: Calculate a child’s height percentile and open the print-friendly growth chart
tasks.description: Use the site’s growth calculator to enter details for an 8-year-old girl with a height of 120 cm, calculate her growth percentile, and then open the print-friendly version of the result.
steps: From the homepage, navigate to the “Tools,” “Growth Charts,” or “Calculators” section using the main navigation or a clearly labeled link.
On the tools page, click on the option labeled “Growth Calculator,” “Height Percentile Calculator,” or similar pediatric growth tool.
In the calculator, select “Female” as the child’s sex from the gender/sex dropdown or radio buttons.
Enter the child’s age as “8” in the age field, making sure the unit is set to years if there is an option between months and years.
Enter the child’s height as “120” in the height field and select “cm” or “centimeters” as the unit if a unit dropdown is present.
If a weight field is optional, leave it blank or unchanged; if required, enter “25” and select “kg” as the unit.
Click the “Calculate,” “View Results,” or similarly labeled button to generate the percentile result.
On the results page, click the link or button labeled “Print-Friendly Version,” “Print Chart,” or similar to open the print-friendly page of the growth chart and results.

tasks.name: Check if a specific insurance plan is accepted and send a billing question
tasks.description: Find out whether the clinic accepts the insurance plan named “BluePlus Silver HMO,” identify any listed co-pay requirement for specialist visits, and submit a billing contact form asking about the co-pay for a new patient visit.
steps: From the homepage, click on the “Billing & Insurance,” “Insurance Information,” or similarly labeled link in the main navigation or footer.
On the insurance page, use the search box or plan list filter to type “BluePlus Silver HMO” and apply the search.
In the search results or plan list, click on the entry for “BluePlus Silver HMO” to open its detailed information page or section.

Scroll through the plan details until you reach the portion describing specialist visit coverage and note the co-pay amount listed for specialist or endocrinology visits (e.g., a dollar amount like $25).
Return to the Billing & Insurance page using the breadcrumb or “Back to Insurance” link, and click on the “Contact Billing,” “Ask a Billing Question,” or similar link.
On the contact form, type “Parent asking about BluePlus Silver HMO co-pay” in the subject field if present.

In the message field, type a short message such as: “Hello, I have BluePlus Silver HMO and want to confirm the co-pay for a new pediatric endocrinology patient visit. The plan details mention a $25 specialist co-pay. Can you confirm this?”

Fill in the contact information fields using the following details: name “Jordan Smith,” phone “555-222-3344,” email “jsmith@example.com,” and click the “Submit,” “Send Message,” or equivalent button to send the question.

tasks.name: Request a refill for levothyroxine 50 mcg to the nearest pharmacy within 5 miles
tasks.description: Use the prescription refill page to request a refill of levothyroxine 50 mcg tablets (30 tablets) for an existing patient and select the first pharmacy within 5 miles of ZIP code 10027, then choose the earliest available pickup time after 4:00 PM today.
steps: From the main navigation or a visible quick link, click on “Prescription Refills” or “Refill a Prescription.”
On the refill page, select “Existing Patient” from the patient type options (such as radio buttons or a dropdown).
In the medication dropdown or text field, choose or type “Levothyroxine 50 mcg tablet” and select it from the suggestions if they appear.
In the quantity field, enter “30” as the number of tablets requested for the refill.
In the pharmacy search section, enter the ZIP code “10027” into the ZIP/postal code field and click the “Search Pharmacies” or similar button.
From the list of pharmacies returned, identify the first pharmacy whose distance is shown as less than or equal to 5 miles and click the “Select,” “Choose,” or radio button for that pharmacy.
In the pickup time section, review the available time slots for today and select the earliest time slot that begins at or after 4:00 PM.

Fill in the contact information fields with: patient name “Noah Green,” date of birth “05/02/2012,” parent/guardian name “Lauren Green,” phone “555-444-7788,” and email “lgreen@example.com,” then click the “Submit Refill Request” button.

tasks.name: Complete a new patient pre-visit questionnaire for early puberty concerns
tasks.description: Access the new patient endocrine intake form for a child with concerns about early puberty, fill in the required demographic and visit-reason fields using specified values, indicate that the upcoming visit date is 05/20/2026, and submit the questionnaire.
steps: From the homepage, navigate to the “Forms & Questionnaires,” “Patient Forms,” or similarly labeled section using the main navigation or a prominent link.
On the forms page, locate and click on the form titled “New Patient Endocrine Intake,” “New Patient Questionnaire,” or similar for endocrine visits.
In the child information section of the form, enter “Sofia Martinez” as the child’s name, “01/18/2016” as the date of birth, and select “Female” as the sex.
In the visit reason or primary concern field, choose or type “Early puberty” from the available options or as free text.
In the parent/guardian section, type “Carlos Martinez” as the parent/guardian name, enter “555-333-1212” as the contact phone number, and “cmartinez@example.com” as the email address.
If there is a question about the referring provider, type “Dr. Karen Lee, Pediatrician” in the referring doctor field.
In the upcoming visit date field, enter “05/20/2026” as the appointment date or select that date from the date picker if provided.

Answer any mandatory background questions by selecting reasonable options (for example, choose “No” for a yes/no question about previous endocrine visits, and choose “None” for an allergies checklist if required), then scroll to the bottom and click the “Submit Form,” “Send Questionnaire,” or equivalent button to complete the form.
